edges.target: Complement Bystander Injury to Oligodendrocytes and Neurons
B Cell and Plasmablast-Mediated AQP4-IgG Production
IL-6-Mediated Th17 Skewing and Blood-Brain Barrier Disruption
B Cell and Plasmablast-Mediated AQP4-IgG Production

nodes.description: Autoantibodies against AQP4 bind astrocytic end-feet and trigger complement-dependent astrocyte damage, driving neuroinflammation. AQP4-IgG is detectable in approximately 80% of NMOSD patients.

evidence.reference_title: Aquaporin-4 in Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorders: A Target of Autoimmunity in the Central Nervous System.
evidence.snippet: In NMOSD, the autoantibody (NMO-IgG) binds to the extracellular loops of AQP4 as expressed in perivascular astrocytic end-feet and disrupts astrocytes in a complement-dependent manner.
Pathognomonic serum autoantibodies against aquaporin-4 (AQP4-IgG, also termed NMO-IgG) are detectable in around 80 % of NMO patients

nodes.description: B cells differentiate into AQP4-IgG-producing plasmablasts peripherally. The pathogenic autoantibody enters the CNS when the blood-brain barrier is permeable and binds AQP4 on astrocytic end-feet. This pathway is the therapeutic target of inebilizumab (anti-CD19) and rituximab (anti-CD20).
nodes.id: B Cell and Plasmablast-Mediated AQP4-IgG Production

evidence.snippet: Peripheral production of the antibody is triggered by an unknown process in genetically susceptible individuals. Anti-AQP4 antibody enters the central nervous system (CNS) when the blood brain barrier is made permeable

nodes.description: Complement activation on AQP4-expressing astrocytes generates soluble activated complement components that diffuse to nearby oligodendrocytes and neurons, depositing membrane attack complex (MAC/C5b-9) on these bystander cells. This complement bystander mechanism explains how AQP4-IgG targeting astrocytes leads to secondary demyelination and neuronal loss without direct antibody binding to oligodendrocytes or neurons.
nodes.id: Complement Bystander Injury to Oligodendrocytes and Neurons

evidence.snippet: we report evidence for a complement 'bystander mechanism' to account for early oligodendrocyte injury in NMO in which activated, soluble complement proteins following AQP4-IgG binding to astrocyte AQP4 result in deposition of the complement membrane attack complex (MAC) on nearby oligodendrocytes

These results support a novel mechanism for early neuron injury in NMO and provide evidence that complement bystander injury may be a general phenomenon for brain cell injury following AQP4-IgG-targeted astrocyte death

nodes.description: Complement C5 inhibitor approved as maintenance therapy for AQP4-IgG seropositive NMOSD.

evidence.reference_title: Recent advances in the treatment of neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorders.
evidence.snippet: Eculizumab, inebilizumab, and satralizumab are effective maintenance therapies approved for the treatment of AQP-4 seropositive NMOSD.

nodes.description: IL-6 promotes differentiation of T cells into Th17 cells, which produce IL-17, a potent pro-inflammatory cytokine that disrupts blood-brain barrier tight junctions and recruits immune cells to sites of CNS inflammation. This pathway is the therapeutic target of satralizumab (anti-IL-6 receptor).
nodes.id: IL-6-Mediated Th17 Skewing and Blood-Brain Barrier Disruption

evidence.snippet: Like other autoimmune diseases, Th17 cells and their effector cytokines (such as interleukin 6) have been implicated in pathogenesis.
IL-6 is essential for the differentiation of T cells into Th17 cells and has been implicated in the initiation and progression of neuroinflammation.

nodes.description: Anti-CD19 B-cell depleting antibody approved as maintenance therapy for AQP4-IgG seropositive NMOSD.

evidence.reference_title: Recent advances in the treatment of neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorders.
evidence.snippet: Eculizumab, inebilizumab, and satralizumab are effective maintenance therapies approved for the treatment of AQP-4 seropositive NMOSD.

nodes.description: Therapeutic plasma exchange (plasmapheresis) used as acute attack treatment for NMOSD, particularly for severe relapses or those refractory to corticosteroids. Removes circulating AQP4-IgG and complement components.

evidence.snippet: The most common treatments used for relapses are steroids and plasma exchange

nodes.description: Anti-CD20 B-cell depleting monoclonal antibody used off-label as maintenance therapy for NMOSD relapse prevention. One of the most commonly used long-term immunosuppressants for NMOSD.

evidence.snippet: long-term NMOSD relapse prevention includes off-label use of immunosuppressants, particularly rituximab

nodes.description: Anti-IL-6 receptor antibody approved as maintenance therapy for AQP4-IgG seropositive NMOSD.

evidence.reference_title: Recent advances in the treatment of neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorders.
evidence.snippet: Eculizumab, inebilizumab, and satralizumab are effective maintenance therapies approved for the treatment of AQP-4 seropositive NMOSD.
